summary: Legendary Morning Dew and a magical La Bamba moment make this show unforgettable; recording source is unknown but fans report no issues.
review: All six reviewers rave about the 1987 performance, singling out an astonishing rendition of "Morning Dew" and the surprise La Bamba jam as highlights. While none comment on the technical side, the consensus is that the music outweighs any recording concerns, delivering one of the top Dew shows of the late‑80s.

ai_rating.rationale: Uniform 4–5 star ratings and enthusiastic language indicate an exceptional show; lack of recording detail keeps rating from being perfect but still very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer mentioned soundboard, audience mix, or audio flaws, implying the recording is at least listenable and does not detract from the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers emphasize that the entire second set after the drums is a "once‑in‑a‑lifetime" stretch, with the Dew and La Bamba providing magical peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his inspired playing on Morning Dew and the overall vibe of the show.
